_comment: Copy to config.json (gitignored) and edit. Every key is optional - server/config.py holds the defaults. Only the keys listed here are accepted; an unknown key is a startup error rather than a silently ignored typo.
_comment_origins: Public URL(s) the site is served on. Used to reject cross-site POSTs. Leave empty to accept only same-origin requests (matched against the Host header).
_comment_registration: registrationOpen=false hides public sign-up entirely; requireInvite=true keeps sign-up open but demands a code from `admin.py invite`. Either way a new account still needs owner approval before it sees anything.
_comment_caps: Bound the daily fetch: it scales with distinct codes, not user count.
_comment_cookie: secureCookie=false ONLY for plain-http local testing. Behind the tunnel it must be true.
_comment_proxy: A tunnel makes every request look like it came from 127.0.0.1, so the real client IP (used for rate limiting) has to be read from a header. Safe only because the server binds loopback and the tunnel is the only thing that can reach it. Set false if anything else can.
_comment_proxy_header: MUST match your tunnel: cloudflared = CF-Connecting-IP, Tailscale Funnel = X-Forwarded-For. Pick a header your tunnel does not overwrite and it arrives exactly as the client typed it - every rate limit below then becomes optional for the attacker.
